meaning: n.联合国经济及社会理事会
examples: "ECOSOC" serves as the central forum for discussing international economic and social issues.
Our proposing of "no-action" motion is in full accordance with the Rules of Procedure adopted by the ECOSOC.
Lastly, the Director-General expressed satisfaction at last year's Annual Ministerial Review (AMR) of ECOSOC on education, to which UNESCO had contributed significantly.
The encounter took place at the Place de Nations prior to the opening of the Annual Ministerial Review of the United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC).
The President further requested UNESCO to contribute to the 2013 thematic discussions of ECOSOC dealing with science and culture, which again had an important link to education.
